full_context: Average normal stress for an axially loaded straight bar under uniform tension or compression: \(\sigma = F/A\). \(\sigma\) is the average normal stress on the cross-section, \(F\) is the axial force (positive for tension, negative for compression), and \(A\) is the cross-sectional area. Valid for linearly elastic materials, small deformations, and uniform stress distribution.

variable_definitions.name: Normal Stress
variable_definitions.meaning: Internal force perpendicular to the cross-section per unit area, representing resistance to tension or compression

variable_definitions.name: Axial Force
variable_definitions.meaning: Tensile or compressive force acting at the centroid of the cross-section

variable_definitions.name: Cross-sectional Area
variable_definitions.meaning: Area of the member cross-section

potential_questions: A circular bar with diameter \(d=10\) mm is subjected to an axial tensile force \(F=5000\) N. Find the normal stress \(\sigma\) on the cross-section.
task_description: 1. Determine the axial force \(F\) acting on the cross-section. 2. Calculate the cross-sectional area \(A\) (for a circle: \(A = \pi d^2/4\)). 3. Compute \(\sigma = F/A\). 4. Output the value and unit of \(\sigma\).

full_context: Definition of normal strain: \(\varepsilon = \Delta L/L\). It is the ratio of the change in length to the original length, describing the relative deformation in the loading direction. Valid for small deformations and linear elasticity.

variable_definitions.name: Normal Strain
variable_definitions.meaning: Ratio of elongation or contraction to original length, describing relative deformation

variable_definitions.name: Elongation or Contraction
variable_definitions.meaning: Change in length due to tension or compression

variable_definitions.name: Original Length
variable_definitions.meaning: Initial length before loading

potential_questions: A bar of original length \(L=2\) m is stretched to \(L'=2.004\) m. Find the normal strain \(\varepsilon\).
task_description: 1. Determine the original length \(L\). 2. Measure the deformed length \(L'\). 3. Calculate the change in length \(\Delta L = L' - L\). 4. Compute \(\varepsilon = \Delta L/L\). 5. Output \(\varepsilon\).

full_context: Hooke’s law for uniaxial tension or compression: \(\sigma = E\varepsilon\). Stress and strain are linearly related in the elastic region, with \(E\) as Young’s modulus. Applies to isotropic, linearly elastic materials under uniaxial stress.

variable_definitions.name: Normal Stress
variable_definitions.meaning: Internal force per unit area perpendicular to the cross-section

variable_definitions.name: Young’s Modulus
variable_definitions.meaning: Ratio of stress to strain in the elastic region, measuring resistance to elastic deformation

variable_definitions.name: Normal Strain
variable_definitions.meaning: Ratio of length change to original length

potential_questions: Steel has modulus \(E=200\) GPa and strain \(\varepsilon=0.001\). Find the stress \(\sigma\).

task_description: 1. Determine Young’s modulus \(E\). 2. Determine the normal strain \(\varepsilon\). 3. Compute \(\sigma = E\varepsilon\). 4. Output \(\sigma\).

discipline.full_context: Average shear stress formula: \(\tau = F_s/A\). \(\tau\) is the tangential internal force per unit area, \(F_s\) is the shear force, and \(A\) is the shear area. Applies when shear stress is approximately uniform over the area.

variable_definitions.name: Shear Stress
variable_definitions.meaning: Internal force parallel to the cross-section per unit area, resisting sliding deformation

variable_definitions.name: Shear Force
variable_definitions.meaning: Force tangent to the cross-section

variable_definitions.name: Shear Area
variable_definitions.meaning: Cross-sectional area resisting shear

potential_questions: A pin in double shear has shear area \(A=50\) mm² and total shear force \(F_s=6000\) N. Find shear stress \(\tau\).
discipline.task_description: 1. Determine the shear force \(F_s\). 2. Determine the shear area \(A\). 3. Compute \(\tau = F_s/A\). 4. Output \(\tau\).

discipline.full_context: Shear Hooke’s law: \(\tau = G\gamma\). Shear stress and shear strain are linearly related in the elastic region, with \(G\) as shear modulus. Applies to small-deformation shear of isotropic, linearly elastic materials.

variable_definitions.name: Shear Stress
variable_definitions.meaning: Internal force per unit area parallel to the cross-section

variable_definitions.name: Shear Modulus
variable_definitions.meaning: Ratio of shear stress to shear strain in elastic shear

variable_definitions.name: Shear Strain
variable_definitions.meaning: Change in right angle of a small element under shear (small angle, dimensionless)

potential_questions: Material has shear modulus \(G=80\) GPa and shear strain \(\gamma=5\times10^{-4}\) rad. Find shear stress \(\tau\).
discipline.task_description: 1. Determine shear modulus \(G\). 2. Determine shear strain \(\gamma\). 3. Compute \(\tau = G\gamma\). 4. Output \(\tau\).

discipline.full_context: Flexure formula for bending normal stress in linearly elastic beams with small deflections: \(\sigma_b = M y / I_z\). Stress is proportional to bending moment \(M\) and distance \(y\) from the neutral axis, and inversely proportional to moment of inertia \(I_z\). Applies to pure bending or bending-dominated loading.

variable_definitions.name: Bending Normal Stress
variable_definitions.meaning: Normal stress in a beam cross-section due to bending

variable_definitions.name: Bending Moment
variable_definitions.meaning: Moment causing bending deformation

variable_definitions.name: Distance from Neutral Axis
variable_definitions.meaning: Vertical distance from the neutral axis to the point of interest

variable_definitions.name: Moment of Inertia
variable_definitions.meaning: Area moment of inertia about the neutral axis, measuring bending resistance

potential_questions: Rectangular beam \(b=20\) mm, \(h=40\) mm, moment \(M=10\) kN·m. Find maximum bending stress \(\sigma_{b,\max}\) at the bottom edge.
discipline.task_description: 1. Determine bending moment \(M\). 2. Calculate moment of inertia \(I_z = b h^3/12\). 3. Find maximum distance \(y_{\max} = h/2\). 4. Compute \(\sigma_{b,\max} = M y_{\max}/I_z\). 5. Output \(\sigma_{b,\max}\).

discipline.full_context: Shear stress distribution in a circular shaft under elastic torsion is linear, maximum at the surface. Shear stress at radius \(\rho\): \(\tau_t = T\rho/J\), where \(T\) is torque, \(\rho\) radial distance, \(J\) polar moment of inertia.

variable_definitions.name: Torsional Shear Stress
variable_definitions.meaning: Shear stress in a shaft cross-section due to torsion

variable_definitions.name: Torque
variable_definitions.meaning: Moment causing torsional deformation

variable_definitions.name: Radial Distance from Center
variable_definitions.meaning: Distance from shaft center to the point of interest

variable_definitions.name: Polar Moment of Inertia
variable_definitions.meaning: Polar moment of area about the shaft center, measuring torsional resistance

potential_questions: Solid shaft \(d=50\) mm, torque \(T=1000\) N·m. Find maximum shear stress \(\tau_{t,\max}\) at the surface.
discipline.task_description: 1. Determine torque \(T\). 2. Calculate polar moment \(J = \pi d^4/32\). 3. Find maximum radius \(\rho_{\max} = d/2\). 4. Compute \(\tau_{t,\max} = T \rho_{\max}/J\). 5. Output \(\tau_{t,\max}\).

discipline.full_context: Axial deformation of a prismatic bar under axial load: \(\Delta l = F L/(E A)\). Applies to linear elasticity, small deformations, constant cross-section, and centric loading.

variable_definitions.name: Axial Deformation
variable_definitions.meaning: Change in length due to axial load

variable_definitions.name: Axial Force
variable_definitions.meaning: Tensile or compressive force on the cross-section

variable_definitions.name: Bar Length
variable_definitions.meaning: Original length before loading

variable_definitions.name: Young’s Modulus
variable_definitions.meaning: Material’s resistance to elastic deformation

variable_definitions.name: Cross-sectional Area
variable_definitions.meaning: Area of the cross-section

potential_questions: Steel bar \(L=2\) m, \(A=100\) mm², \(E=200\) GPa, load \(F=10\) kN. Find elongation \(\Delta l\).
discipline.task_description: 1. Determine axial force \(F\). 2. Determine original length \(L\). 3. Determine Young’s modulus \(E\). 4. Determine cross-sectional area \(A\). 5. Compute \(\Delta l = F L/(E A)\). 6. Output \(\Delta l\).

discipline.full_context: Angle of twist for a circular shaft under elastic torsion: \(\varphi = T L/(G J)\). \(\varphi\) is the relative rotation between two cross-sections. Applies to uniform shafts, small deformations, linear elastic shear.

variable_definitions.name: Angle of Twist
variable_definitions.meaning: Relative rotation between two cross-sections under torsion

variable_definitions.name: Torque
variable_definitions.meaning: Applied twisting moment

variable_definitions.name: Shaft Length
variable_definitions.meaning: Length of the segment under torque

variable_definitions.name: Shear Modulus
variable_definitions.meaning: Material’s resistance to elastic shear deformation

variable_definitions.name: Polar Moment of Inertia
variable_definitions.meaning: Polar moment of area about the shaft center

potential_questions: Solid steel shaft \(d=40\) mm, \(L=1.5\) m, \(G=80\) GPa, torque \(T=500\) N·m. Find twist angle \(\varphi\).
discipline.task_description: 1. Determine torque \(T\). 2. Determine shaft length \(L\). 3. Determine shear modulus \(G\). 4. Calculate polar moment \(J = \pi d^4/32\). 5. Compute \(\varphi = T L/(G J)\). 6. Output \(\varphi\).

discipline.full_context: Definition of section modulus (bending resistance): \(W_z = I_z / y_{\max}\). Measures the bending resistance of a cross-section. Often used for maximum bending stress: \(\sigma_{\max} = M/W_z\).

variable_definitions.name: Section Modulus
variable_definitions.meaning: Geometric parameter measuring bending resistance

variable_definitions.name: Moment of Inertia
variable_definitions.meaning: Area moment of inertia about the neutral axis

variable_definitions.name: Maximum Distance from Neutral Axis
variable_definitions.meaning: Farthest distance from neutral axis to cross-section edge

potential_questions: Rectangular beam \(b=30\) mm, \(h=60\) mm. Find section modulus \(W_z\).
discipline.task_description: 1. Calculate moment of inertia \(I_z = b h^3/12\). 2. Find maximum distance \(y_{\max} = h/2\). 3. Compute \(W_z = I_z/y_{\max}\). 4. Output \(W_z\).

discipline.full_context: Maximum Normal Stress Theory (1st strength theory): Brittle fracture occurs when the maximum principal stress reaches the material strength. Equivalent stress: \(\sigma_{\mathrm{eq1}} = \sigma_1\). Used for brittle materials.

variable_definitions.name: Equivalent Stress (1st Theory)
variable_definitions.meaning: Predicts brittle fracture using maximum tensile stress

variable_definitions.name: Maximum Principal Stress
variable_definitions.meaning: Algebraically largest principal stress at a point

potential_questions: Principal stresses \(\sigma_1=80\) MPa, \(\sigma_2=30\) MPa, \(\sigma_3=-20\) MPa, allowable stress \([\sigma]=100\) MPa. Check using 1st theory.
discipline.task_description: 1. Identify principal stresses \(\sigma_1, \sigma_2, \sigma_3\). 2. Take maximum principal stress \(\sigma_1\). 3. Set equivalent stress \(\sigma_{\mathrm{eq1}} = \sigma_1\). 4. Compare \(\sigma_{\mathrm{eq1}} \le [\sigma]\).

discipline.full_context: Maximum Shear Stress Theory (3rd strength theory): Yielding occurs when maximum shear stress reaches the yield limit. Equivalent stress: \(\sigma_{\mathrm{eq3}} = \sigma_1 - \sigma_3\). Used for ductile materials.

variable_definitions.name: Equivalent Stress (3rd Theory)
variable_definitions.meaning: Predicts yielding using maximum shear stress

variable_definitions.name: Maximum Principal Stress
variable_definitions.meaning: Algebraically largest principal stress

variable_definitions.name: Minimum Principal Stress
variable_definitions.meaning: Algebraically smallest principal stress

potential_questions: Principal stresses \(\sigma_1=120\) MPa, \(\sigma_2=60\) MPa, \(\sigma_3=0\) MPa, allowable \([\sigma]=150\) MPa. Check using 3rd theory.
discipline.task_description: 1. Identify principal stresses \(\sigma_1, \sigma_2, \sigma_3\). 2. Find \(\sigma_1\) and \(\sigma_3\). 3. Compute \(\sigma_{\mathrm{eq3}} = \sigma_1 - \sigma_3\). 4. Check \(\sigma_{\mathrm{eq3}} \le [\sigma]\).

discipline.full_context: Distortion Energy Theory (4th strength theory, equivalent to von Mises yield criterion for isotropic materials): Yielding occurs when the distortion energy reaches a critical value. Equivalent stress is given by the formula shown.

variable_definitions.name: Equivalent Stress (4th Theory)
variable_definitions.meaning: Predicts yielding using distortion energy

variable_definitions.name: Maximum Principal Stress
variable_definitions.meaning: Algebraically largest principal stress

variable_definitions.name: Intermediate Principal Stress
variable_definitions.meaning: Middle principal stress

variable_definitions.name: Minimum Principal Stress
variable_definitions.meaning: Algebraically smallest principal stress

potential_questions: Principal stresses \(\sigma_1=100\) MPa, \(\sigma_2=50\) MPa, \(\sigma_3=-30\) MPa, allowable \([\sigma]=120\) MPa. Check using 4th theory.
discipline.task_description: 1. Identify principal stresses \(\sigma_1, \sigma_2, \sigma_3\). 2. Compute squared differences. 3. Sum and divide by 2. 4. Take square root for \(\sigma_{\mathrm{eq4}}\). 5. Check \(\sigma_{\mathrm{eq4}} \le [\sigma]\).

discipline.full_context: Euler’s critical buckling load for a slender column: \(F_{\mathrm{cr}} = \pi^2 E I/(\mu L)^2\). Applies to long, slender, linearly elastic columns under small-deflection stability analysis. \(\mu\) is the end-condition factor.

variable_definitions.name: Critical Buckling Load
variable_definitions.meaning: Maximum axial load before elastic buckling

variable_definitions.name: Young’s Modulus
variable_definitions.meaning: Material’s elastic stiffness

variable_definitions.name: Moment of Inertia
variable_definitions.meaning: Area moment of inertia about the bending axis

variable_definitions.name: End Condition Factor
variable_definitions.meaning: Coefficient reflecting support conditions

variable_definitions.name: Column Length
variable_definitions.meaning: Actual length of the column

potential_questions: Hinged-end steel column \(L=3\) m, diameter \(d=50\) mm, \(E=200\) GPa. Find critical load \(F_{\mathrm{cr}}\).
discipline.task_description: 1. Determine end condition factor \(\mu=1\) for pinned-pinned. 2. Calculate moment of inertia \(I = \pi d^4/64\). 3. Substitute into \(F_{\mathrm{cr}} = \pi^2 E I/(\mu L)^2\). 4. Output \(F_{\mathrm{cr}}\).

discipline.full_context: Axial stiffness (equivalent spring constant) of a prismatic bar: \(k = E A/L\). Force required to produce unit axial deformation.

variable_definitions.name: Axial Stiffness
variable_definitions.meaning: Resistance to axial deformation, force per unit deformation

variable_definitions.name: Young’s Modulus
variable_definitions.meaning: Material elastic stiffness

variable_definitions.name: Cross-sectional Area
variable_definitions.meaning: Area of the bar cross-section

variable_definitions.name: Bar Length
variable_definitions.meaning: Length of the member

potential_questions: Steel bar \(L=2\) m, \(A=100\) mm², \(E=200\) GPa. Find axial stiffness \(k\).
discipline.task_description: 1. Determine Young’s modulus \(E\). 2. Determine area \(A\). 3. Compute \(E A\). 4. Determine length \(L\). 5. Compute \(k = E A / L\). 6. Output \(k\).

discipline.full_context: Equivalent bending stiffness at the free end of a cantilever beam: deflection \(\delta = P L^3/(3 E I)\), so stiffness \(k = P/\delta = 3 E I/L^3\).

variable_definitions.name: Cantilever Tip Bending Stiffness
variable_definitions.meaning: Force per unit deflection at the free end

variable_definitions.name: Young’s Modulus
variable_definitions.meaning: Material elastic stiffness

variable_definitions.name: Moment of Inertia
variable_definitions.meaning: Area moment of inertia about neutral axis

variable_definitions.name: Beam Length
variable_definitions.meaning: Length from fixed to free end

potential_questions: Cantilever steel beam \(L=1.5\) m, \(b=20\) mm, \(h=40\) mm, \(E=200\) GPa. Find tip stiffness \(k\).
discipline.task_description: 1. Calculate \(I = b h^3/12\). 2. Compute \(3 E I\). 3. Compute \(L^3\). 4. Compute \(k = 3 E I / L^3\). 5. Output \(k\).

discipline.full_context: Torsional stiffness of a circular shaft: \(T = (G J/L)\varphi\), so stiffness \(k = T/\varphi = G J/L\). Applies to uniform shafts and linear elastic shear.

variable_definitions.name: Torsional Stiffness
variable_definitions.meaning: Torque per unit angle of twist

variable_definitions.name: Shear Modulus
variable_definitions.meaning: Material shear stiffness

variable_definitions.name: Polar Moment of Inertia
variable_definitions.meaning: Polar moment of area

variable_definitions.name: Shaft Length
variable_definitions.meaning: Length of the segment

potential_questions: Solid steel shaft \(d=40\) mm, \(L=2\) m, \(G=80\) GPa. Find torsional stiffness \(k\).
discipline.task_description: 1. Calculate \(J = \pi d^4/32\). 2. Compute \(G J\). 3. Compute \(k = G J / L\). 4. Output \(k\).

discipline.full_context: Average bearing stress between a pin and a hole, approximated using the pin cross-section \(A = \pi d^2/4\), so \(\sigma_c = F/A = 4F/(\pi d^2)\). Applies to uniform contact and linear elastic approximation.

variable_definitions.name: Bearing Stress
variable_definitions.meaning: Contact pressure per unit area for strength check

variable_definitions.name: Bearing Force
variable_definitions.meaning: Force acting on the contact surface

variable_definitions.name: Pin Diameter
variable_definitions.meaning: Diameter of the pin

potential_questions: Pin diameter \(d=10\) mm, bearing force \(F=5\) kN. Find bearing stress \(\sigma_c\).
discipline.task_description: 1. Determine bearing force \(F\). 2. Determine pin diameter \(d\). 3. Calculate area \(A = \pi d^2/4\). 4. Compute \(\sigma_c = 4F/(\pi d^2)\). 5. Output \(\sigma_c\).

discipline.full_context: Maximum shear stress at the surface of a solid circular shaft under elastic torsion. Derived from \(\tau_{\max} = T \rho_{\max}/J\) with \(\rho_{\max}=d/2\) and \(J=\pi d^4/32\).

variable_definitions.name: Maximum Torsional Shear Stress
variable_definitions.meaning: Peak shear stress at the outer surface of a solid shaft

variable_definitions.name: Torque
variable_definitions.meaning: Applied twisting moment

variable_definitions.name: Shaft Diameter
variable_definitions.meaning: Diameter of the solid shaft

potential_questions: Solid shaft \(d=50\) mm, torque \(T=2000\) N·m. Find maximum shear stress \(\tau_{\max}\).
discipline.task_description: 1. Determine torque \(T\). 2. Determine shaft diameter \(d\). 3. Compute \(\tau_{\max} = 16T/(\pi d^3)\). 4. Output \(\tau_{\max}\).

discipline.full_context: Maximum bending normal stress at the outer fiber of a beam: \(\sigma_{\max} = M/W_z\). Consistent with \(\sigma_b = M y/I_z\) because \(W_z = I_z/y_{\max}\).

variable_definitions.name: Maximum Bending Normal Stress
variable_definitions.meaning: Stress at the farthest point from the neutral axis

variable_definitions.name: Bending Moment
variable_definitions.meaning: Moment causing bending

variable_definitions.name: Section Modulus
variable_definitions.meaning: Geometric parameter for bending resistance

potential_questions: Beam section modulus \(W_z=8\times10^{-6}\) m³, moment \(M=2\) kN·m. Find \(\sigma_{\max}\).
discipline.task_description: 1. Determine bending moment \(M\). 2. Determine section modulus \(W_z\). 3. Compute \(\sigma_{\max} = M/W_z\). 4. Output \(\sigma_{\max}\).
